2011-12-01.title: Dutch Seek Space for a Growing Appetite for Pork
2011-12-01.abstract: The Netherlands is a small country with a lot of pigs — and growing friction over how and where to raise them.

2011-12-01.title: Money Flows, but What Euro Zone Lacks Is Glue
2011-12-01.abstract: In Europe there are growing concerns that even an aggressive intervention by the European Central Bank will not be enough to stabilize the markets and preserve the euro.

2011-12-01.title: Outsourcing Giant Finds It Must Be Client, Too
2011-12-01.abstract: India’s new private sector is increasingly powering the economy, but it chafes at what many companies say are labor laws that blunt hiring and stifle growth.

2011-12-01.title: Islamists Say They Have Mandate in Egypt Voting
2011-12-01.abstract: Winning a dominant majority in Egypt’s first Parliament since the ouster of Hosni Mubarak would be the religious movement’s most significant step since the start of the Arab Spring.

2011-12-01.title: Obama Refrains From a Formal ‘I’m Sorry’ to Pakistan
2011-12-01.abstract: The White House has for now overruled State Department officials who favored a show of remorse to help salvage relations after a NATO airstrike killed Pakistani soldiers.

2011-12-02.title: In Egypt, No Alliance With Ultraconservatives, Islamist Party Says
2011-12-02.abstract: The Muslim Brotherhood’s political arm distanced itself from another Islamist party in Egypt as early tallies indicated they would claim the two largest roles in Parliament.

2011-12-02.title: French President Warns of Dire Consequences if Euro Crisis Goes Unsolved
2011-12-02.abstract: In a televised speech, President Nicolas Sarkozy told his countrymen that Europe risks being “swept away” by the ongoing euro crisis if it fails to change course.

2011-12-02.title: Raids Don’t Keep Tunnel City From Humming Underground
2011-12-02.abstract: Tunnels built to move drugs into the United States have become an always-operating part of life in Tijuana, as entrenched as cheap pharmacies and strip clubs.

2011-12-02.title: 18 Months After Vote, Belgium Has Government
2011-12-02.abstract: The financial markets prodded political parties to agree on a governing coalition, but the prime minister-to-be, Elio Di Rupo, has not mastered Dutch, the majority language.

2011-12-02.title: For Afghan Woman, Justice Runs Into Unforgiving Wall of Custom
2011-12-02.abstract: A pardon for an Afghan woman who was imprisoned for adultery after she reported being raped came with the expectation that she would marry her attacker.

2011-12-03.title: International Prosecutor Seeks Sudanese Official’s Arrest in Darfur Violence
2011-12-03.abstract: If the International Criminal Court issues a warrant, Defense Minister Abdel Rahim Muhammad Hussein will be the country’s fourth senior politician wanted in connection to violence in Darfur.

2011-12-03.title: After American Jewish Outcry, Israel Ends Ad Campaign Aimed at Expatriates
2011-12-03.abstract: An effort to appeal to Israeli patriotism struck a sour note with American Jews.

2011-12-03.title: From Ceremonial Figure to Italy’s Quiet Power Broker
2011-12-03.abstract: President Giorgio Napolitano, 86, last month capped a distinguished career by orchestrating one of the most complex political transfers in Italian postwar history.

2011-12-03.title: Defense Chief Says Israel Must Mend Arab Ties
2011-12-03.abstract: Defense Secretary Leon E. Panetta spoke sternly to America’s closest ally in the Middle East, telling Israel it must take “bold action” — diplomatic, not military — to mend ties with its Arab neighbors.

2011-12-03.title: After an Explosion in Iraq, Competing Ideas About the Target
2011-12-03.abstract: Iraqi officials disagreed over whether the intended victim of a bombing in Baghdad was Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki or Osama al-Najafi, the speaker of Parliament.

2011-12-04.title: Myanmar’s Courts Subvert Rule of Law, Activists Say
2011-12-04.abstract: A lawyer leading a campaign against land confiscation by government officials and their business associates is facing what supporters say are politically motivated charges.

2011-12-04.title: U.S. Agents Launder Mexican Profits of Drug Cartels
2011-12-04.abstract: D.E.A. agents have laundered millions in drug proceeds because it helps them identify how cartels move money, where they keep it and who their leaders are.

2011-12-04.title: Film’s Focus on a Flawed Russian Figure Brings Ire With Putin Into View
2011-12-04.abstract: A documentary on the saga of the jailed former oil magnate Mikhail Khodorkovsky, who for some has become a symbol of opposition to Vladimir V. Putin, officially premiered in Moscow on Friday.

2011-12-04.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2011-12-04.abstract: Top magazine and long-form articles this week.

2011-12-04.title: Japan Poll Finds Record Good Will for U.S.
2011-12-04.abstract: A record 82 percent of Japanese report having friendly feelings toward the United States, results attributed to the American military’s humanitarian effort after the March tsunami.

2011-12-05.title: Leaders Piece Together an Effort to Keep the Euro Intact
2011-12-05.abstract: European leaders are working overtime on a tentative deal to try to save the euro, which they hope to complete at a crucial summit meeting this week.

2011-12-05.title: Explosion Seen as Big Setback to Iran’s Missile Program
2011-12-05.abstract: A huge blast that destroyed a major missile-testing site three weeks ago was a major blow to Iran’s most advanced long-range missile program, experts and officials said.

2011-12-05.title: For Turkey, Lure of Tie to Europe Is Fading
2011-12-05.abstract: With Europe roiled by a debt crisis and the tumult of the Arab Spring creating opportunities for Turkey to wield new clout, Turks are weighing walking away from the European Union.

2011-12-05.title: Biden, in Visits to Middle East and Europe, Tries to Reassure Allies of U.S. Support
2011-12-05.abstract: In his travels abroad, Vice President Joseph R. Biden Jr. has preached a consistent message of commitment — even if some American policies suggest otherwise.

2011-12-05.title: U.S. Tightens Its Security in Baghdad
2011-12-05.abstract: Threats of kidnapping and terrorist attacks are cited as American forces continue their withdrawal from Iraq.

2011-12-06.title: Lawmakers to Open an Inquiry on Undercover Drug Operation
2011-12-06.abstract: Republicans said they wanted more information on a Drug Enforcement Administration case in which agents laundered and smuggled drug money.

2011-12-06.title: Twists in a South Africa Corruption Case Prove Telling
2011-12-06.abstract: The plight of a former national police commissioner sparks a question in South Africa: Is the country heading toward a more equal society? Or does patronage still reign supreme?

2011-12-06.title: Tensions Rise in Congo’s Capital as Ballots Pile Up
2011-12-06.abstract: Congo’s protracted electoral aftermath is settling into a volatile mix of accusation and counteraccusation, warnings, tension and panic.

2011-12-06.title: Party’s Losses Raise Concerns About Putin’s Bid
2011-12-06.abstract: Waning support for United Russia could endanger the presidential prospects of Prime Minister Vladimir V. Putin.

2011-12-06.title: Aid Agencies in Afghanistan Fear Reversals After U.S. Exit
2011-12-06.abstract: Civilian aid workers worry that Afghanistan will be abandoned and that the hard-earned development gains will be reversed.

2011-12-07.title: U.S. and South Korea Renew Talks on Nuclear Technology
2011-12-07.abstract: The question for Washington is whether the South, unlike the North, should be allowed to enrich uranium and reprocess spent nuclear fuel.

2011-12-07.title: Greenpeace Leader Visits Boardroom, Without Forsaking Social Activism
2011-12-07.abstract: Kumi Naidoo’s approach to revitalizing Greenpeace, a 40-year-old organization with something of an identity crisis, has brought him plenty of detractors.

2011-12-07.title: Dip in Consumer Spending Slows Growth in Brazil
2011-12-07.abstract: Economists say Brazil’s growth in 2011 will slow to about 3 percent or lower, a sharp reversal from a booming 2010.

2011-12-07.title: Sectarian Violence Worsens in Homs
2011-12-07.abstract: In one of the worst episodes of sectarian violence in Syria since the uprising began nine months ago, dozens of corpses were recovered from the streets of Homs this week.

2011-12-07.title: Merkel’s Path: Brinkmanship for Debt Crisis
2011-12-07.abstract: Chancellor Angela Merkel of Germany appears to have adopted a strategy aimed at remaking the euro zone in her country’s likeness.

2011-12-08.title: China: Hundreds Arrested on Child-Selling Charges
2011-12-08.abstract: More than 600 people across China have been arrested on suspicion of selling children, according to a statement released Wednesday by the Ministry of Public Security.

2011-12-08.title: Drone Crash in Iran Reveals Secret U.S. Surveillance Effort
2011-12-08.abstract: High-altitude flights of stealth C.I.A. drones from bases in Afghanistan had been among the most secret of many American intelligence-collection efforts against Iran.

2011-12-08.title: Drone Crash in Iran Reveals Secret U.S. Surveillance Program
2011-12-08.abstract: Times reporters Scott Shane and David E. Sanger write about the implications of an exposed C.I.A. drone program, which had been among the most covert American intelligence-collection efforts against Iran.

2011-12-08.title: Libyan Civilians Hold on to a Deadly Legacy
2011-12-08.abstract: Citizens are reluctant to give up the guns and other weapons they seized as they wrested control of the country from Qaddafi loyalists.

2011-12-08.title: Pakistani President Is Said to Be in Dubai for Heart Tests
2011-12-08.abstract: Asif Ali Zardari suddenly left for Dubai on Tuesday evening, leading to talk that he was being forced to resign by the country’s powerful military.

2011-12-09.title: Libya Will Allow British Police to Visit for Lockerbie Inquiry, Briton Says
2011-12-09.abstract: Libya’s leaders signaled new flexibility in prosecuting the Libyan perpetrators of the Lockerbie bombing and the killing of a London policewoman during the 1980s, British officials said.

2011-12-09.title: A Nod to Willing Flesh Entangled in Dispiriting Words

2011-12-09.title: A Wartime Leader Ends a War He Never Wanted

2011-12-09.title: Factional Splits Hinder Drive to Topple Syria Leader
2011-12-09.abstract: Tensions are rising between a rebel army and Syria’s main opposition group over the group’s insistence that the rebels limit themselves to defensive action.

2011-12-09.title: Honduras: Killings Prompt a Ban on Motorcycles Carrying Passengers
2011-12-09.abstract: After two high-profile killings this week, legislators approved a decree late Wednesday banning motorcyclists from riding with passengers.

2011-12-10.title: Afghanistan: Suicide Bomber Kills 6 Security Officers at Prayer
2011-12-10.abstract: A suicide bomber in eastern Afghanistan killed six Afghan security officials, including a district police chief, during Friday Prayer in Ghaziabad District.

2011-12-10.abstract: The American forces still in Iraq are continuing their patrols, scouring for explosives and trying to ensure that departing convoys leave safely.

2011-12-10.title: Iran Complains to Security Council About Spy Drone
2011-12-10.abstract: Iran, which has said it captured an advanced reconnaissance plane, accused the United States of hostile and aggressive behavior.

2011-12-10.abstract: Members of the European Union installed rules that would make it harder for them to take on high levels of debt without penalty.

2011-12-11.title: On Russian TV, a Straightforward Account Is Startling
2011-12-11.abstract: Government television covered the protests much as they had occurred — to the surprise of many.

2011-12-11.title: Climate Talks in Durban Yield Limited Agreement
2011-12-11.abstract: A conference in South Africa ended with the promise to work toward a new global treaty in coming years and the establishment of a new climate fund.

2011-12-11.abstract: Salomón Lerner, Peru’s cabinet chief, quit unexpectedly after failing to resolve a dispute over an ambitious and controversial gold-mining project.

2011-12-11.abstract: A decade of growth under Vladimir V. Putin, now Russia’s prime minister, has created a class of wealthy urban professionals who are not afraid to express their political frustration.

2011-12-12.abstract: Manuel Antonio Noriega arrived from Paris to complete a 20-year sentence for three murders and possibly face further court action.

2011-12-12.abstract: Pakistan had ordered the C.I.A. out of the Shamsi air base in protest over NATO airstrikes that killed at least 25 Pakistani soldiers.

2011-12-12.abstract: The Obama administration is wrestling with a decision about whether to turn Ali Musa Daqduq over to the Iraqi government or to find a way to take him with the military as it withdraws.

2011-12-12.abstract: The Afghan ambassador promised to look into circumstances that Iran has said ended with its recovery of a C.I.A. spy plane.

2011-12-13.abstract: Israel on Monday closed a rickety footbridge leading to a holy compound revered by Jews and Muslims after a city engineer determined that it posed an immediate danger.

2011-12-14.abstract: Inquiries into a Lebanon bank have offered evidence of an international money-laundering apparatus that funds the group, also exposing possible direct involvement of Hezbollah officials in the drug trade.

2011-12-15.abstract: In an attempt to reframe the conversation about the region, 11 acclaimed artists are exploring its fault lines.

2011-12-15.abstract: Mr. Whitman, the American-born owner of Shakespeare & Company in Paris, believed that “the book business is the business of life.”

2011-12-17.abstract: Lori Berenson was in prison for more than 15 years on a conviction of collaborating with a group that planned to attack the Peruvian Congress.

2011-12-17.abstract: After years of fighting, Houthis are expressing a willingness to participate in a new political process.

2011-12-18.abstract: The villager, who was trying to resolve a land dispute between local governments and residents, died in police custody.

2011-12-18.abstract: The convoy’s departure came three days after the American military folded its flag in a ceremony to celebrate the end of its mission and marked the end of the nearly nine-year war.

2011-12-19.abstract: The report says the man is an Iranian-American from Arizona. The claims could not be verified, and the C.I.A. declined to comment.

2011-12-19.abstract: A tense showdown between Pakistan’s powerful army and its besieged civilian government brought President Asif Ali Zardari hurrying back from Dubai early on Monday.

2011-12-20.abstract: Questions loom about Kim Jong-un’s ability to manage North Korea’s ravaged economy, with its chronic shortages and deprivations, to avoid a complete collapse.

2011-12-20.abstract: For South Korean and American intelligence services to have failed to pick up clues right away about Kim Jong-il’s death attests to the secretive nature of North Korea.

2011-12-20.abstract: President Christian Wulff has faced calls for his resignation after being accused of misleading state lawmakers about a home loan.

2011-12-20.abstract: Many described the singing and dancing at protests as just part of the uprising’s cultural flowering.

2011-12-20.abstract: Under threat of sanctions and increasing international pressure, the government of President Bashar al-Assad will allow observers to monitor pledges to end the violent crackdown.

2011-12-21.abstract: Pictures of mourning from North Korea show a blend of compulsory emotion, cultural expressions of loss and sincere, spontaneous outbursts of grief.

2011-12-21.abstract: Scenes of mass hysteria and grief among North Koreans appeared to be part of an official campaign to build support for Kim Jong-il’s successor, his third son, Kim Jong-un.

2011-12-21.abstract: Despite emerging as Latin America’s rising power, Brazil still trails its neighbors in prosecuting officials for crimes that include murder, disappearance and torture.

2011-12-21.abstract: The Communist Party is trying to convince Russians that it has a plan to move the country forward and does not simply want to revive the Soviet Union.

2011-12-21.abstract: The rial tumbled to its lowest level ever against the dollar in panic selling caused by increased economic isolation, inflation and worries about government solutions.

2011-12-22.abstract: The increasing drug and organized-crime violence in Central America has led the Peace Corps to pull out of Honduras and stop sending new volunteers to Guatemala and El Salvador.

2011-12-22.abstract: Influential officials in the latter part of Kim Jong-il’s rule.

2011-12-22.abstract: Tens of thousands of women in at least a half-dozen countries received the implants that were made with substandard silicone and that have been rupturing at unusually high rates.

2011-12-23.title: How to Control Libya Missiles? Buy Them Up
2011-12-23.abstract: Talks between the United States and Libya are the latest effort to account for thousands of portable antiaircraft missiles missing after rebels overran arms depots.

2011-12-23.abstract: Vice President Tariq al-Hashimi, facing an arrest warrant on terrorism charges, said reconciliation with Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki, a Shiite, “might be impossible.”

2011-12-23.abstract: President Michel Martelly will not perform again as his alter ego, Sweet Micky, after all, an adviser said Thursday.

2011-12-23.abstract: After a land-dispute protest which attracted media from around the world, life in Wukan, China, seems to have returned to normal for now.

2011-12-24.abstract: A critical mass of young Russians decided this month that they had the power to alter the course of political events.

2011-12-24.abstract: Friday’s protest against military rule was the largest outbreak of demonstrations outside the capital since Nov. 18.

2011-12-24.abstract: The country’s supreme governing body agreed to release the prisoners but said that those convicted of serious crimes would not be part of the amnesty.

2011-12-25.title: Suicide Bomber Strikes Funeral in Afghanistan

2011-12-25.abstract: Prime Minister Yoshihiko Noda of Japan arrived in Beijing for a two-day state visit that is expected to focus on the uncertainty over North Korea’s intentions after the death of Kim Jong-il.

2011-12-25.abstract: The death of Khalil Ibrahim represents an enormous blow to the Justice and Equality Movement, which has played a major role in the rebellion in Darfur.

2011-12-25.abstract: Boko Haram, a radical Muslim sect waging an increasingly sophisticated sectarian fight, claimed a series of attacks.

2011-12-26.title: A Village in Revolt Could Be a Harbinger for China
2011-12-26.abstract: At least 625,000 small, locally run villages in China frequently suffer the same sorts of injustices that prompted the outburst this month in Wukan.

2011-12-26.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI, appearing frail and tired, prayed for peace in the Middle East and Horn of Africa in his annual message, “Urbi et Orbi,” or “to the city and world,” from St. Peter’s Basilica.

2011-12-26.abstract: A plea to Kim Jong-un to take command of the armed forces, along with a noticeable military presence, suggest that the succession is proceeding smoothly.

2011-12-26.abstract: Los Angeles native Alex Cutler left behind a life of American affluence to follow his dream in the funk music scene of Rio’s gritty slums.

2011-12-27.title: Fiscal Crisis Takes Toll on Health of Greeks
2011-12-27.abstract: A 13 percent cut in health care spending as part of widespread austerity measures has left public hospitals lacking personnel and supplies, and many Greeks without care.

2011-12-27.title: Greece’s Deep Cuts
2011-12-27.abstract: As Greece is forced to make steep spending cuts, many families are falling into poverty and face losing healthcare coverage.

2011-12-27.title: Kazakhstan: President Fires Son-in-Law
2011-12-27.abstract: President Nursultan Nazarbayev of Kazakhstan, who has appointed several relatives to government jobs, fired his son-in-law from one of them: manager of the country’s sovereign wealth fund.

2011-12-27.title: Afghanistan to Disband Irregular Police Force Set Up Under NATO
2011-12-27.abstract: The decision appeared aimed at stopping at least some of the militias that are beyond the control of President Karzai and could one day challenge the government.

2011-12-27.title: Shifting Debate Over Lokpal
2011-12-27.abstract: A look at politicians’ and parties’ changing views in recent months.

2011-12-28.title: Making Themselves Heard in a Changed Iraq
2011-12-28.abstract: Andrea Bruce, a New York Times photographer, peered into the lives of Iraqis over the last months of the war and captured quietly powerful portraits that show optimism and resilience.

2011-12-28.title: Karzai Agrees to Let Taliban Set Up Office in Qatar
2011-12-28.abstract: After prodding by his Western allies, President Hamid Karzai said that Afghanistan would accept a Taliban office in Qatar for the purposes of peace negotiations.

2011-12-28.title: Rapes on the Rise in Somalia
2011-12-28.abstract: This year, tens of thousands in Somalia have died from famine, with countless others cut down in relentless combat. Now Somalis face yet another widespread terror: an alarming increase in rapes and sexual abuse of women and girls.

2011-12-28.title: For Somali Women, Pain of Being a Spoil of War
2011-12-28.abstract: With the famine putting hundreds of thousands of women on the move, more rapes are being reported now in Somalia than at any time in recent memory.

2011-12-28.title: Israeli Girl, 8, at Center of Tension Over Religious Extremism
2011-12-28.abstract: Naama Margolese has become the face of a conflict over the influence of extremists in Israel after her story of being accosted by ultra-Orthodox men was reported in the media.

2011-12-29.title: A Haven for Dissent in Bahrain, Where Lattes and Tear Gas Mix
2011-12-29.abstract: With no place to legally organize, activists and opposition leaders have made Costa Coffee an unlikely gathering place.

2011-12-29.title: Toxic Coffee in Bahrain
2011-12-29.abstract: A coffee shop serves as a makeshift command center for some opposition leaders of Bahrain's stumbling pro-democracy movement. As a result, police often attack it with tear gas.

2011-12-29.title: Violence Flares in Syria Despite Observers’ Visit
2011-12-29.abstract: Human rights advocates have questioned whether the observers have the qualifications or enough independence from the government to help end a conflict that many fear is veering toward civil war.

2011-12-29.title: Weapons Sales to Iraq Move Ahead Despite U.S. Worries
2011-12-29.abstract: Some fear that American weapons will strengthen Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki’s apparent efforts to consolidate his power and establish Shiite dominance.

2011-12-29.title: China: Strike at a Korean Factory
2011-12-29.abstract: About 8,000 workers at an electronics factory in Nanjing are on strike over what they call low wages from their South Korean employers, according to an advocacy group.

2011-12-30.title: Spain: King’s Son-in-Law Summoned to Court Over Fraud Allegations
2011-12-30.abstract: A high court in Spain issued a summons to the son-in-law of King Juan Carlos as a suspect in a fraud scandal that involves millions of dollars in public money given to his private foundation.

2011-12-30.title: Disputed Voting Turns Church, a Kremlin Ally, Into Its Critic
2011-12-30.abstract: After disputed elections, the Russian Orthodox Church, including Patriarch Kirill I, has defended protests and could play a major role as the political and social crisis unfolds.

2011-12-30.title: Italian Premier Outlines Plan to Stimulate Growth in a Struggling Economy
2011-12-30.abstract: Prime Minister Mario Monti’s proposals include liberalizing Italy’s closed professions and guilds, encouraging competitiveness and modernizing the country’s outdated infrastructure.

2011-12-30.title: Chaos at Midnight, Hung Lokpal
2011-12-30.abstract: A controversial anti-corruption bill suspended until February.

2011-12-30.title: Shooting Dharavi, A Photographer’s View
2011-12-30.abstract: Photojournalist Adam Ferguson on shooting in India’s biggest slum.

2011-12-31.title: In Libya, Modified Weapon Becomes Less of a Threat
2011-12-31.abstract: A little video from the Libyan war has a few nonproliferation heads nodding with grudging approval as it shows a converted rocket launcher that could never be fired at planes.

2011-12-31.title: Organic? Yes. Sustainable? Not Always.
2011-12-31.abstract: More Americans are buying foods with the organic label, but the products are increasingly removed from the traditional organic ideal of locally grown on small farms in a way that protects the environment.

2011-12-31.title: Hunt for Gas Hits Fragile Soil, and South Africans Fear Risks
2011-12-31.abstract: A plan to drill for natural gas in the Karoo region of South Africa would use millions of gallons of water in a drought-stricken area.

2011-12-31.title: Kurds Protest Airstrike Killing Dozens in Turkey
2011-12-31.abstract: Kurds took to the streets a day after the Turkish authorities admitted that airstrikes in northern Iraq intended to kill Kurdish militants had instead claimed the lives of 35 civilians.

2011-12-31.title: China: Village’s Ballots Canceled
2011-12-31.abstract: Officials in Guangdong Province canceled the results of recent elections in Wukan, which recently evicted all its Communist Party officials over a land dispute, according to the state news agency.
